_note: Updated by the game systems designer after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Define the base movement system — jump, dash, and wall mechanics with exact values
priorities.note: All level design depends on the movement parameters being locked. Gap widths, platform heights, and challenge construction are all derived from the movement system. These values need to be finalized before level design can commit to room geometry.
priorities.proposed_solution: Lock jump, dash, wall mechanics, and numeric parameters in living balance docs so level design can size gaps and platforms.

priorities.title: Specify the first ability the player acquires and how it opens the world
priorities.note: The first gating ability defines the whole progression structure. Needs Game Director alignment on the player arc before this can be decided.
priorities.proposed_solution: After macro arc alignment, specify the first ability the player earns and exactly which locks and routes it opens.

risks_p1.title: Movement parameters are not yet locked
risks_p1.note: Level design cannot finalize room geometry until jump height, dash distance, and wall-jump behavior are committed to. This is the critical path blocker.

actions.name: Ability Spec
actions.description: Complete spec for a new ability — inputs, effects, cost, cooldown, frame parameters, edge cases, balance baseline.
actions.trigger: A new ability is proposed or approved for development

actions.name: Balance Model
actions.description: Parameterized balance model with derived values — no arbitrary number assignments.
actions.trigger: A system is under design or post-playtesting data is available

actions.name: Feel Audit
actions.description: Compare current movement or combat parameters against design targets — specific deltas and recommended corrections.
actions.trigger: Movement or combat reported as feeling off

actions.name: System Interaction Map
actions.description: Map interactions between a new mechanic and existing systems — second-order effects, conflicts, required adjustments.
actions.trigger: A new mechanic is proposed that touches existing systems

opportunities.title: Draft the combat system fundamentals — attack frames, iframes, enemy hitbox conventions
opportunities.note: Held until movement system is locked. Combat parameters should be derived from the same base model, not designed in isolation.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Derive combat frame data, iframes, and hitbox conventions from the locked movement model rather than inventing them in isolation.

opportunities.title: Map out how abilities interact with each other to prevent accidental broken combinations
opportunities.note: As abilities are added, interaction effects compound. Building a system interaction map early prevents edge cases from becoming game-breaking sequence breaks later.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Maintain an ability interaction map as mechanics are added so unintended sequence breaks are caught early.
